title: 🌐 Deploying an AWS VPC with Terraform (Hands-On Lab) — Part 8
summary: This hands-on guide walks through building a complete AWS VPC with Terraform, covering subnet creation and internet gateway setup. It demonstrates the fundamental AWS networking architecture that forms the foundation for most AWS deployments.
content:
So far in this series, we’ve:
Built Terraform fundamentals
Created reusable modules
Managed remote state
Designed production-ready structure
Compared workspaces vs environments
Now it’s time to build something real 🔥
👉 A complete AWS VPC using Terraform
In this guide:
What a VPC is
How to design basic network architecture
Create VPC using Terraform
Add subnet and internet gateway
A VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) is:
👉 Your own isolated network in AWS
It allows you to control:
IP range
Subnets
Routing
Internet access
VPC (10.0.0.0/16)
│
├── Public Subnet (10.0.1.0/24)
│   └── Internet Gateway

vpc-lab/
  main.tf
  variables.tf

provider "aws" {
  region = "ap-southeast-1"
}

resource "aws_vpc" "main" {
  cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16"

  tags = {
    Name = "terraform-vpc"
  }
}

resource "aws_subnet" "public" {
  vpc_id     = aws_vpc.main.id
  cidr_block = "10.0.1.0/24"

  tags = {
    Name = "public-subnet"
  }
}

resource "aws_internet_gateway" "igw" {
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id

  tags = {
    Name = "main-igw"
  }
}

resource "aws_route_table" "public" {
  vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id
}

resource "aws_route" "internet_access" {
  route_table_id         = aws_route_table.public.id
  destination_cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0"
  gateway_id             = aws_internet_gateway.igw.id
}

resource "aws_route_table_association" "a" {
  subnet_id      = aws_subnet.public.id
  route_table_id = aws_route_table.public.id
}

terraform init
terraform plan
terraform apply

You now have:
A VPC
A public subnet
Internet access via IGW
Routing configuration
👉 This is the foundation of AWS networking.
Almost every AWS architecture starts with:
👉 VPC → Subnets → Routing
Always clean up resources:
terraform destroy

👉 Avoid unnecessary AWS cost.
Basic AWS networking
Terraform resource relationships
Real infrastructure deployment
This is no longer theory.
👉 You are now building real cloud infrastructure.
Next, we go deeper:
👉 Build a 3-tier architecture (ALB + EC2 + DB)
Hi, I’m Ahkar — sharing

zh.title: 🌐 使用 Terraform 部署 AWS VPC（实践实验）— 第 8 部分
zh.summary: 本实践指南演示了如何使用 Terraform 构建完整的 AWS VPC，涵盖子网创建和互联网网关设置。它演示了形成大多数 AWS 部署基础的基本 AWS 网络架构。

title: Show the current audit configuration, including log path, retention, rotation, and compression settings with nylas audit conf...
summary: The nylas audit config show command displays all active audit settings including log paths, retention periods, rotation, and compression options. It's essential for organizations verifying SOC 2 compliance requirements and overseeing AI agent activity.
content:
Show the current audit configuration, including log path, retention, rotation, and compression settings. Essential for SOC 2 compliance and AI agent oversight.
The nylas audit config show command displays every audit setting in effect — log file path, retention period, max file size, daily rotation, compression, and whether request IDs and API details are recorded.  Use it to verify your audit setup before enabling logging or to troubleshoot why logs aren't appearing where expected.
nylas audit config show

Reach for nylas audit config show for compliance reporting, AI agent oversight, or debugging automation failures. Combine with --json to pipe output into other tools.
Display current audit configuration:
nylas audit config show

Check configuration as JSON for scripting:
nylas audit config show --json

Every entry in the audit log captures: the exact command, all arguments (with secrets redacted), the invoker identity (human or AI agent name), the exit code, and a nanosecond timestamp. This level of detail is what compliance auditors expect.
Full docs: nylas audit config show reference — all flags, advanced examples, and troubleshooting.
All commands: Nylas CLI Command Reference
Get started: brew install nylas/nylas-cli/nylas — other install methods

zh.title: 显示当前审计配置，包括日志路径、保留期、轮换和压缩设置（nylas audit config show）
zh.summary: nylas audit config show 命令显示所有活跃的审计设置，包括日志路径、保留期、轮换和压缩选项。这对于验证 SOC 2 合规要求和监督 AI 代理活动的组织至关重要。

title: Using nylas audit logs disable to disable audit logging. Stops recording CLI activity to the audit log file
summary: The nylas audit logs disable command turns off audit logging while preserving existing log data. Organizations use this to control when new CLI activity records are written to the audit log file.
content:
When AI agents send emails on your behalf, you need a record of what happened. nylas audit logs disable is part of that audit trail.
The nylas audit logs disable command turns off audit logging so the CLI no longer writes activity records to the log file.  Existing log data is preserved — only new entries stop being written.
Disable audit logging:
nylas audit logs disable

Disable and verify status:
nylas audit logs disable
nylas audit logs status

nylas audit logs disable

Every entry in the audit log captures: the exact command, all arguments (with secrets redacted), the invoker identity (human or AI agent name), the exit code, and a nanosecond timestamp. This level of detail is what compliance auditors expect.
Logs still being written after disable
Command not found
brew upgrade nylas-cli or re-run the install script.
Full docs: nylas audit logs disable reference — all flags, advanced examples, and troubleshooting.
All commands: Nylas CLI Command Reference
Get started: brew install nylas/nylas-cli/nylas — other install methods

zh.title: 使用 nylas audit logs disable 禁用审计日志。停止将 CLI 活动记录到审计日志文件
zh.summary: nylas audit logs disable 命令关闭审计日志记录，同时保留现有日志数据。组织使用此命令来控制何时将新的 CLI 活动记录写入审计日志文件。

title: nylas audit logs enable: Developer Reference with Examples
summary: The nylas audit logs enable command activates audit logging to record all CLI actions to a local file for compliance and debugging purposes. By default, logs capture command details, arguments with secrets redacted, invoker identity, and nanosecond timestamps.
content:
Compliance and debugging both need logs. nylas audit logs enable handles the enable audit logging. records all cli activity to a local log file for compliance and debugging.
The nylas audit logs enable command activates audit logging, recording every CLI action — email sends, calendar reads, auth changes — to a local file.  By default logs are written to ~/.
brew install nylas/nylas-cli/nylas
nylas init
nylas audit logs enable

nylas audit logs enable [--output PATH]

Every entry in the audit log captures: the exact command, all arguments (with secrets redacted), the invoker identity (human or AI agent name), the exit code, and a nanosecond timestamp. This level of detail is what compliance auditors expect.
Run nylas audit logs enable --help to see all available flags. Add --json for machine-readable output — useful when piping into jq or feeding data to scripts.
nylas audit logs enable --help

Enable audit logging to default location:
nylas audit logs enable

Enable logging to a custom file:
nylas audit logs enable --output /var/log/nylas-audit.log

nylas audit init — Initialize audit logging for the Nylas CLI. Creates the log directory, default c
nylas audit logs show — View and filter audit log entries. Supports filtering by date range, command, st
nylas audit logs summary — View summary statistics of audit log entries including command counts, error rat
nylas audit logs clear — Clear all audit log entries. This is a destructive action — consider exporting l
Full docs: nylas audit logs enable reference — all flags, advanced examples, and troubleshooting.
All commands: Nylas CLI Command Reference
Get started: brew install nylas/nylas-cli/nylas — other install methods

zh.title: nylas audit logs enable: 开发者参考与示例
zh.summary: nylas audit logs enable 命令激活审计日志，将所有 CLI 操作记录到本地文件用于合规和调试。默认情况下，日志会捕获命令详情、隐蔽的机密参数、调用者身份和纳秒级时间戳。

title: nylas audit logs status Explained — Flags, Examples, Tips
summary: The nylas audit logs status command reports whether audit logging is active and displays the current log file location, size, and entry count. It helps teams verify logging is working correctly or locate the log file for compliance review.
content:
Check whether audit logging is enabled and show the current log file location. Essential for SOC 2 compliance and AI agent oversight.
The nylas audit logs status command reports whether audit logging is active, the path to the current log file, its size on disk, and the number of entries recorded.  Use it to confirm logging is working or to find the log file for review.
nylas audit logs status

Every entry in the audit log captures: the exact command, all arguments (with secrets redacted), the invoker identity (human or AI agent name), the exit code, and a nanosecond timestamp. This level of detail is what compliance auditors expect.
Check audit logging status:
nylas audit logs status

Get status as JSON for monitoring scripts:
nylas audit logs status --json

Combine with other commands: Chain nylas audit logs status with other Nylas CLI commands using shell pipes and variables for complex workflows.
Debug mode: Add --verbose to see the underlying API requests and responses — useful when something doesn't behave as expected.
nylas audit init — Initialize audit logging for the Nylas CLI. Creates the log directory, default c
nylas audit logs show — View and filter audit log entries. Supports filtering by date range, command, st
nylas audit logs summary — View summary statistics of audit log entries including command counts, error rat
nylas audit logs clear — Clear all audit log entries. This is a destructive action — consider exporting l
Full docs: nylas audit logs status reference — all flags, advanced examples, and troubleshooting.
All commands: Nylas CLI Command Reference
Get started: brew install nylas/nylas-cli/nylas — other install methods

zh.title: nylas audit logs status 详解 — 标志、示例、技巧
zh.summary: nylas audit logs status 命令报告审计日志是否启用，并显示当前日志文件的位置、大小和条目数。它帮助团队验证日志是否正常工作，或定位用于合规审查的日志文件。

title: Show DST transition dates for a timezone, including the next spring-forward and fall-back dates — nylas timezone dst Guide
summary: The nylas timezone dst command displays Daylight Saving Time transition dates for any IANA timezone, showing spring-forward and fall-back timestamps with UTC offset changes. It works entirely offline with no API calls or internet connection required.
content:
Timezone operations that work offline, with zero API calls. nylas timezone dst runs entirely on your machine.
The nylas timezone dst command displays Daylight Saving Time transition dates for any IANA timezone.  It shows the next spring-forward and fall-back timestamps, the UTC offset before and after each transition, and how many minutes shift.
brew install nylas/nylas-cli/nylas
nylas init
nylas timezone dst

nylas timezone dst --zone TIMEZONE

Check US Eastern DST transitions:
nylas timezone dst --zone America/New_York

Check EU DST transitions:
nylas timezone dst --zone Europe/London

JSON output for scripting:
nylas timezone dst --zone Asia/Tokyo --json

These timezone commands use the IANA timezone database compiled into the binary. No network calls, no API keys, no rate limits. They work on airplanes.
Unknown timezone error
nylas timezone list to see all valid identifiers.
No DST transitions shown
Full docs: nylas timezone dst reference — all flags, advanced examples, and troubleshooting.
All commands: Nylas CLI Command Reference
Get started: brew install nylas/nylas-cli/nylas — other install methods

zh.title: 显示时区的 DST 转换日期，包括下一次春季前进和秋季后退日期 — nylas timezone dst 指南
zh.summary: nylas timezone dst 命令显示任何 IANA 时区的夏令时转换日期，包括春季前进和秋季后退时间戳及 UTC 偏移变化。它完全离线运行，无需 API 调用或互联网连接。

title: nylas timezone find-meeting in Practice: Find overlapping business hours across multiple timezones to schedule a meeting
summary: The nylas timezone find-meeting command calculates overlapping business hours across multiple timezones to identify available meeting windows. It uses the IANA timezone database compiled into the binary, requiring no network calls or API keys.
content:
Find overlapping business hours across multiple timezones to schedule a meeting. No internet connection or API key needed.
The nylas timezone find-meeting command calculates available meeting windows when participants span multiple timezones.  Pass two or more IANA zones and an optional duration, and it returns time slots where all participants fall within business hours (9 AM - 5 PM local).
nylas timezone find-meeting --zones ZONE1,ZONE2 [--duration MINUTES]

Find meeting time for US and EU teams:
nylas timezone find-meeting --zones America/New_York,Europe/London

Find 60-minute slot across three timezones:
nylas timezone find-meeting --zones America/Los_Angeles,America/New_York,Asia/Kolkata --duration 60

These timezone commands use the IANA timezone database compiled into the binary. No network calls, no API keys, no rate limits. They work on airplanes.
Reach for nylas timezone find-meeting when scheduling meetings across time zones or converting times in scripts. Combine with --json to pipe output into other tools.
Script-friendly: Add --json for machine-readable output and --yes (where supported) to skip confirmations in automated pipelines.
CI/CD ready: This command works in non-interactive mode. Set NYLAS_API_KEY as an environment variable and it picks up credentials automatically.
Full docs: nylas timezone find-meeting reference — all flags, advanced examples, and troubleshooting.
All commands: Nylas CLI Command Reference
Get started: brew install nylas/nylas-cli/nylas — other install methods

zh.title: Nylas Timezone Find-Meeting 实践：跨多个时区查找重叠的工作时间来安排会议
zh.summary: Nylas timezone find-meeting 命令可计算跨多个时区的重叠工作时间，以找出可用的会议时间窗口。它使用编译到二进制文件中的 IANA 时区数据库，无需网络调用或 API 密钥。

title: Every MCP Tool Call My AI Makes Now Gets a Signed Receipt
summary: Agent Receipts is an open protocol that creates Ed25519-signed, hash-chained receipts for every MCP tool call, providing verifiable evidence of agent actions. It operates as a signing proxy that wraps any MCP server without requiring ongoing trust in the proxy.
content:
When an AI agent calls a tool via MCP (the protocol agents use to interact with external services), it tells you what it did. But that's a claim — not evidence. The logs are self-reported. There's no independent proof of what was actually sent or received.
Agent Receipts is an open protocol that puts a signing proxy between the MCP client and server. Every tool call gets an Ed25519-signed, hash-chained receipt — a W3C Verifiable Credential you can independently verify without trusting the proxy after the fact.
I routed the GitHub MCP server through it, found two bugs during the session, and filed both bug reports — through the proxy. The act of reporting the bugs is itself receipted. The dogfooding loop closed.
It's early days (proxy v0.3.3), open source, ships as a single Go binary, and wraps any MCP server — not just GitHub. Open issues in security review, protocol design, and DX if you want to help shape it.
Full walkthrough: Every MCP Tool Call My AI Makes Now Gets a Signed Receipt
Repo: github.com/agent-receipts/ar

zh.title: 我的 AI 现在对每个 MCP 工具调用都获得了签名收据
zh.summary: Agent Receipts 是一个开放协议，为每个 MCP 工具调用创建 Ed25519 签名的哈希链式收据，提供代理操作的可验证证据。它作为签名代理运行，可包装任何 MCP 服务器，无需对代理持续信任。

title: I tried Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern on my AWS consulting work — here's what changed
summary: The author applied Andrej Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern to rebuild an AWS consulting knowledge base, using an LLM to maintain a persistent, cross-linked wiki instead of relying on retrieval-augmented generation. This approach prevents knowledge decay and enables the LLM to handle bookkeeping rather than re-deriving answers from raw sources on every query.
content:
I tried Karpathy's LLM Wiki principle on my own AWS consulting work a few weeks ago, and I can't go back.
If you missed it, Andrej Karpathy published a short gist called LLM Wiki: A Pattern for Persistent Knowledge Bases. His thesis: instead of using RAG to re-derive answers from raw sources on every query, let the LLM maintain a persistent, compounding wiki. Humans curate sources and ask questions. The model handles the bookkeeping — summarizing, cross-linking, keeping pages consistent over time.
So I tried it. I rebuilt my entire AWS consulting knowledge base around Karpathy's three-layer pattern. This post is what I actually did, what the folder looks like now, the frontmatter schema that makes it queryable by Claude Code skills, and the three rules I had to enforce to stop it from rotting.
At the bottom there's a copy-pasteable Claude Code prompt that scaffolds the same structure in an empty directory — if you want to try it yourself.
Every engagement I run has the same four information streams, and all four used to decay fast:
Client-specific context — their account structure, compliance posture, team, kickoff constraints.
Cross-project AWS patterns — cross-account alarm routing, IAM boundaries, cost allocation.
Decisions — why I picked Aurora over RDS for this client, what tradeoff I accepted.
Gotchas — what broke, in what AWS service, under what conditions.
Before Karpathy's pattern, all four lived, well not in the same place let's say. Every new engagement brought the same conclusion that I was the bottleneck :).
Now the LLM compiles it once and keeps it current for me. I'm not the bottleneck anymore.
./raw/
├── assets/                      # Downloaded images, screenshots
├── aws-reinvent-2024-obs.md     # Clipped article
├── client-kickoff-2026-03.md    # Meeting transcript
├── datadog-quote-client-a.pdf   # Vendor docs
└── aws-bill-client-b-q1.csv     # Cost exports

Raw never changes. If the wiki layer gets corrupted or I change conventions, I can re-der

zh.title: 我在 AWS 咨询工作中尝试了 Karpathy 的 LLM Wiki 模式——这里是变化
zh.summary: 作者将 Andrej Karpathy 的 LLM Wiki 模式应用于重建 AWS 咨询知识库，使用 LLM 维护持久的、交叉链接的维基，而不是依赖检索增强生成。这种方法可防止知识衰减，并使 LLM 能够处理簿记，而不是每次查询时从原始源重新推导答案。

title: Revisiting Idris in 2026: Is It Ready for Real Work?
summary: Idris 2 has matured into a stable, production-ready language for correctness-critical code where type-checking enforces invariants at compile time. It's best suited for specialized domains like parsers, DSLs, and compilers rather than as a general-purpose replacement for mainstream languages.
content:
Idris has always promised something bold: encode correctness directly into the type system. In 2026, with Idris 2 firmly established, it’s worth revisiting whether that promise translates into production-grade reality.
Short answer: yes, in the right places.
Idris 1
Proof-of-concept era
Slow compiler, heavy memory usage
Research-focused ergonomics
Effectively legacy
Idris 2
Complete reimplementation
Faster compilation and smaller core
Multiple backends (Chez Scheme default, C available)
Actively maintained and stable
If you’re evaluating Idris today, Idris 2 is the only sensible choice.
Idris doesn’t just type-check values — it type-checks facts.
data Vect : Nat -> Type -> Type where
  Nil  : Vect 0 a
  (::) : a -> Vect n a -> Vect (S n) a

Concatenation proves length correctness:
append : Vect n a -> Vect m a -> Vect (n + m) a
append Nil       ys = ys
append (x :: xs) ys = x :: append xs ys

No runtime checks. The compiler enforces the invariant.
total
head : Vect (S n) a -> a
head (x :: _) = x

This function cannot be called on an empty vector. Invalid states are unrepresentable.
Compiler & performance: Significantly faster than Idris 1, reasonable compile times
Error messages: Improved structure, still proof-oriented and verbose
Tooling: LSP support exists, interactive editing is powerful, docs are uneven
Usable, but not luxurious.
useOnce : (1 x : a) -> a
useOnce x = x

Linear types enable compile-time guarantees around resource usage: no double-use, no leaks, no implicit copying.
Protocols and state machines
Parsers, DSLs, validation-heavy logic
Compilers and interpreters
Correctness-critical business logic
Small ecosystem
Hiring challenges
Interop overhead
Not ideal for fast-moving CRUD apps
Idris works best as a correctness core, not a default full-stack choice.
Idris isn’t trying to replace Go, Rust, or TypeScript.
It’s for teams asking:
What if the compiler enforced our invariants?
What if tests were secondary to types?
In 2026, Idris 2 is stable enough for

zh.title: 2026年重新审视Idris：它已准备好用于实际工作吗？
zh.summary: Idris 2已成熟为一个稳定的、生产就绪的语言，用于关键性代码的正确性，其中类型检查在编译时强制执行不变量。它最适合用于解析器、DSL和编译器等专业领域，而不是作为主流语言的通用替代品。

title: How Do You Debug What You Can't Reproduce? An IPv6 case study in minimal test environments
summary: This case study demonstrates debugging a customer's IPv6 environment issue without fully replicating the exact production setup by focusing on minimal reproducible examples that isolate the specific conditions causing the bug. It applies the scientific method to systematic debugging when exact environment replication isn't feasible.
content:
The Problem


A customer reported that their Discovery scans were failing in a pure IPv6 environment. The error messages pointed to two different issues:
Scenario A: LocationParseError: Failed to parse - when using raw IPv6 addresses
Scenario B: OSError: [Errno 101] Network is unreachable - when using FQDNs
The tricky part? The customer was running a single-stack IPv6 OpenShift cluster - a rare configuration typically seen in telecommunications environments on bare-metal infrastructure. This wasn't a cluster we could just spin up on the fly. Setting one up would require specialized OpenShift expertise and dedicated resources, neither of which were readily available to us.
The Challenge: You Can't Always Replicate the Customer's Environment
Convert an existing cluster to IPv6? 
Before diving into the solution, it's worth stepping back to consider what we're actually doing when we debug. Debugging isn't just trial and error - it's a systematic process with well-established principles.
The Scientific Method in Debugging
Observe: Gather evidence (error messages, logs, customer reports)
Hypothesize: Form a theory about what's causing the issue
Predict: Determine what you'd expect to see if your hypothesis is correct
Test: Create an experiment to validate or invalidate the hypothesis
Iterate: Refine your hypothesis based on results
In our case, we had two hypotheses for Scenario B:
Hypothesis 1: The Quadlet-managed container network lacks IPv6 routes
Hypothesis 2: Python's socket library behaves differently than curl under certain conditions
Both needed testing - but we couldn't test them without a reproducible environment.
The Minimal Reproducible Example (MRE)
Minimal: Contains only what's necessary to reproduce the issue
Reproducible: Can be reliably repeated
Example: Demonstrates the specific problem
The key insight is that you don't need to replicate the customer's entire environment. You need to replicate the conditions that cause the bug. Everything else is noise.

zh.title: 如何调试无法重现的问题？IPv6在最小化测试环境中的案例研究
zh.summary: 本案例研究展示了如何在不完全复制确切生产环境的情况下调试客户的IPv6环境问题，通过关注最小化可重现示例来隔离导致错误的特定条件。当无法复制确切环境时，它将科学方法应用于系统调试。

title: LiteLLM got hacked. I built a simpler LLM gateway you can actually audit.
summary: LiteLLM versions 1.82.7-1.82.8 suffered a supply chain attack in March 2026 with malicious code including credential harvesters and backdoors, affecting 95 million monthly downloads. The author created FreeLLM, a simpler OpenAI-compatible gateway focusing on multi-provider routing to address LiteLLM's complexity and opacity concerns.
content:
On March 24, 2026, LiteLLM versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 were uploaded to PyPI with a credential harvester, a Kubernetes lateral-movement toolkit, and a persistent remote code execution backdoor baked in.
The malicious package was live for about 40 minutes before PyPI quarantined it.
40 minutes doesn't sound like much. But LiteLLM gets 95 million downloads a month. It's the default multi-provider routing library for anyone building on LLMs. Teams running pip install litellm during that window got compromised automatically. No explicit import needed. The payload triggered on Python interpreter startup via a .pth file.
Google brought in Mandiant for the investigation. Snyk, Kaspersky, and Trend Micro all published breakdowns. The attack vector: a compromised Trivy security scanner leaked CircleCI credentials, including the PyPI publishing token and a GitHub PAT.
This is not a theoretical risk. This happened.

LiteLLM does a lot. 2,000+ models across 100+ providers. Proxy server, load balancing, spend tracking, A/B testing, caching, logging, guardrails, prompt management.
That scope is the problem.
A developer on HN described the codebase as having a 7,000+ line utils.py. A 30-year engineer called it "the worst code I have ever read in my life." Before the supply chain attack, a DEV Community post titled "5 Real Issues With LiteLLM That Are Pushing Teams Away in 2026" was already documenting the trust erosion.
The supply chain attack was the tipping point, not the root cause. The root cause is depending on a massive, opaque library for critical routing infrastructure.
I ran into the same multi-provider routing problem last year while building Metis, an AI stock analysis tool. Kept burning through Groq's free tier in 20 minutes, switching to Gemini manually, hitting their cap, switching again.
Built FreeLLM to stop doing that manually. It solves a narrower problem than LiteLLM, and that's the point.
FreeLLM is an OpenAI-compatible gateway that routes across Groq, Gemini, M

zh.title: LiteLLM被黑客入侵。我构建了一个更简单的你可以实际审计的LLM网关。
zh.summary: LiteLLM版本1.82.7-1.82.8在2026年3月遭遇供应链攻击，包含凭证收集器和后门等恶意代码，影响了9500万月度下载量。作者创建了FreeLLM，一个更简单的OpenAI兼容网关，专注于多提供商路由，以解决LiteLLM的复杂性和不透明性问题。
